sanskrit: यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते । एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
hindiTranslation: सांख्ययोगियोंके द्वारा जो तत्त्व प्राप्त किया जाता है, कर्मयोगियोंके द्वारा भी वही प्राप्त किया जाता है ।  अतः जो मनुष्य सांख्ययोग और कर्मयोगको फलरूपमें एक देखता है, वही ठीक देखता है ॥
englishTranslation: The state reached by the wise is also reached through yoga. One who sees wisdom and yoga as one sees rightly.
essence: Different paths can lead to the same freedom.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The wise path of understanding and the path of disciplined action lead to the same realization. Seeing them as separate is the mistake.
simpleInsight: You can stop ranking paths and relax into what they both accomplish.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna is untangling a false split. He says the state reached by clear understanding is also reached through disciplined action, so the two are not rivals.
modernRelevance: You are stuck between quitting and pushing harder, as if only one choice can be right. This verse cuts through that panic: different methods can lead to the same settled place.
translationLiteral: यत् (that which) / सांख्यैः (by the wise of analysis) / प्राप्यते (is attained) / स्थानं (state) / तत् (that) / योगैः (by yogis) / अपि (also) / गम्यते (is reached) / एकं (one) / सांख्यं (analysis) / च (and) / योगं (yoga) / च (and) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / सः (that one) / पश्यति (sees rightly)
insight: It shifts the mind from arguing over methods to recognizing the shared result beneath them.

quoteVariants.text: Wisdom and yoga reach the same place. Seeing them as one is clear seeing.
The path differs; the arrival does not.
One truth. Two paths. Same arrival.
